evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my Cordova app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyApp.js, and shows the setup checklist (plugins to install, cordova prepare, camera permissions, CSP, data-capture-view DOM element).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions cordova plugin add scandit-cordova-datacapture-core and scandit-cordova-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions cordova prepare after plugin install
Setup checklist mentions CSP meta tag or that iOS/Android camera permissions are auto-configured by the plugins
Setup checklist mentions a sized DOM element (e.g. <div id="data-capture-view">) is required for the camera preview
document.addEventListener('deviceready' is used as the init gate
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
A license key placeholder string is present (semantic)
new Scandit.BarcodeCapture( is present (v8 constructor used)
Scandit.BarcodeCapture.forContext( is NOT present
context.setMode( or context.addMode( is called to register the mode with the context
Scandit.Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Scandit.Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies except EAN13UPCA and Code128 are enabled
Scandit.DataCaptureView.forContext( is present
view.connectToElement( is called
new Scandit.BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present
view.addOverlay( is called
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called to register the scan listener
Listener object has a didScan callback
camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.On) is called
The Scandit.* global is used (no 'import ... from scandit-cordova-datacapture-*' statements in runtime code)

evals.prompt: Add BarcodeCapture barcode scanning to ProductListApp.js. When a barcode is scanned, add it to the scannedProducts array and re-render the product list.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeCapture to ProductListApp.js alongside the existing DOM rendering code. All existing DOM hooks, state, and render functions are preserved.

assertions.text: document.addEventListener('deviceready' is present
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new Scandit.BarcodeCapture( is present
context.setMode( or context.addMode( is called
Scandit.DataCaptureView.forContext( is present
view.connectToElement( is present
new Scandit.BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present
view.addOverlay( is present
barcodeCapture.addListener( is present
didScan callback is present
session.newlyRecognizedBarcode is present
didScan callback pushes / appends to scannedProducts
Existing scannedProducts array is preserved
Existing renderProductList function is preserved and called after each scan
Existing total / totalLabel DOM update logic is preserved
Existing clearButton click handler is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my Cordova app, I want to disable the capture mode while a result modal is shown, then re-enable it when the user taps OK. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false inside the didScan callback, shows a result UI, and re-enables the mode (barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true) when the user dismisses the modal.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside the didScan callback
A result UI element is shown (DOM creation, classList toggle, or a dialog)
An OK / dismiss handler re-enables scanning by setting barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress duplicate scans of the same barcode within 500 milliseconds. Update my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance and re-applies the settings to the BarcodeCapture mode if needed.

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter = 500 is set (or equivalent with the local variable name used)
codeDuplicateFilter is set before constructing BarcodeCapture, or barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings) is called after the change (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a rectangular viewfinder to my BarcodeCapture overlay so users see a clear scan area on screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a RectangularViewfinder to overlay.viewfinder.

assertions.text: overlay.viewfinder is assigned a new Scandit.RectangularViewfinder( instance
Scandit.RectangularViewfinderStyle is referenced (e.g. Square, Rounded)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Customize the BarcodeCapture feedback so a successful scan vibrates but does not play a sound.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a BarcodeCaptureFeedback with a Feedback whose sound is null and vibration is the default vibration, then assigns it to barcodeCapture.feedback.

assertions.text: Scandit.BarcodeCaptureFeedback.defaultFeedback is referenced (or new Scandit.BarcodeCaptureFeedback() is constructed)
feedback.success is assigned a new Scandit.Feedback( with Vibration.defaultVibration and null sound (or equivalent)
barcodeCapture.feedback = feedback is assigned
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when the user leaves the scan page in my Cordova app — disable the BarcodeCapture mode and switch the camera off so battery isn't drained.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires teardown code into the page-exit path: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false and camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.Off).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set in the teardown path
camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.Off) is called in the teardown path
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to restrict scanning to barcodes near the centre of the camera frame so that bystander barcodes don't get scanned. Use a radius location selection.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a RadiusLocationSelection on settings.locationSelection and re-applies the settings.

assertions.text: settings.locationSelection is assigned a Scandit.RadiusLocationSelection (or equivalent location selection)
barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings) is called after the change, or the change is made before new Scandit.BarcodeCapture(settings) (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Switch BarcodeCapture from the default Smart scan intention to Manual so the user has to deliberately aim at each barcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.scanIntention = Scandit.ScanIntention.Manual and re-applies the settings to the BarcodeCapture instance.

assertions.text: settings.scanIntention = Scandit.ScanIntention.Manual is set
barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings) is called after the change, or the change is made before new Scandit.BarcodeCapture(settings) (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable scanning of GS1 Composite Code Type A and Type B in my BarcodeCapture integration.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the symbologies needed for the requested composite types and sets enabledCompositeTypes on the settings.

assertions.text: settings.enableSymbologiesForCompositeTypes( is called with Scandit.CompositeType.A and Scandit.CompositeType.B
settings.enabledCompositeTypes is assigned an array containing Scandit.CompositeType.A and Scandit.CompositeType.B
barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings) is called after the change, or the change is made before new Scandit.BarcodeCapture(settings) (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved
